response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer whose career has been almost entirely built on TypeScript, Node.js, and JavaScript ecosystems. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project — nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development this role demands. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge, and his one adjacent finance role (RIDE Capital) was focused on Node/TypeScript/GraphQL rather than any relevant financial systems. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience, nor any Windows platform development. The profile is too far removed from the mandatory requirements to warrant consideration.
strengths: Strong CI/CD and DevOps background with GitLab, Docker, and multiple CI pipeline setups across roles
Demonstrated Java development experience, fulfilling one of the desirable optional skills
Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, conference talks, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a thesis project, far short of the required 5–10 years
No capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial-systems domain knowledge evident anywhere in the resume
No SQL Server or Oracle experience mentioned; the candidate's database background is MongoDB, MySQL, and generic SQL — not the enterprise RDBMS platforms required

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
